## [00:00 - 03:12]

Hi, this is Don Crowther. Let's talk about driving traffic to your webinar registration page. So there are what we call owned traffic versus traffic that you don't own. In this case, owned traffic is traffic where you control the messaging. So your blog, social media, things where you're controlling what you're saying there as opposed to other people controlling it. So examples, emails to your list. Note that's plural. You can have lots of emails sending people to your webinar registration page. I recommend a number of them. Next, blog posts. Again, plural. How about if I just not point out to you the plurals and all of these. Every visitor to your site or blog should see a promo for your webinar during your webinar period. And you can do that by literally running ads on your own site. So the equivalent if you're advertising somebody else's product, why not advertise your own? You can also do this with popups on your site, as well as top and bottom bars running on the top and the bottom of your website. You can do this through your podcast, through push notifications. So if you have one of those push notification things that puts a little bar at the top that says, hey, do you want to receive push notifications from this particular site? And if they click yes, then you're sending out a notification that says, hey, we got a cool webinar coming. Next is text notifications. So if you have their text address, I wouldn't do this a lot, but every once in a while, it's a great way to promote. I've got a webinar coming up. I've seen physical mailings work where someone is promoting a product or a conference, sending out a physical mailing inviting people to come to a webinar. And hey, just talking with people as well as calling people who should be there. I bet you if you really looked at your list in your audience, you could probably identify 10 to 15 people who really ought to be on your next webinar. Well, pick up the phone and call them. You don't own posts on other people's social media groups and forums, but you might be able to get permission or make a post that will send people into your webinar.

## [03:12 - 03:33]

And there's of course paid advertising. So all of these are elements that you should be considering and capitalizing on to drive people to your webinar registration page so that you can get people into the top of your webinar funnel.
